items.title: DexCompose: Reusing Dexterous Policies for Multi-Task Manipulation with a Single Hand

items.title: Which Nash Equilibrium? Solver-Dependent Selection on Zero-Sum Nash Polytopes
items.summary: Many two-player zero-sum games admit not a unique Nash equilibrium but a convex set of them: a polytope of profiles that all share the minimax value V* yet prescribe different behaviour. items.title: Agentic Hardware Design as Repository-Level Code Evolution
items.summary: We present HORIZON, a self-evolving agent framework that treats hardware design as repository-level code evolution. items.title: Learning Topology-Aware Representations via Test-Time Adaptation for Anomaly Segmentation

items.title: How Width and Data Shape Generalization Scaling Laws in Quadratic Neural Networks

items.title: Govern the Repository, Not the Agent: Measuring Ecosystem-Level Risk in AI-Native Software

items.title: Exposure Bias Can Alleviate Itself via Directional and Frequency Rectification in Flow Matching
items.summary: Flow Matching (FM) has achieved remarkable generative performance, yet it suffers from exposure bias due to discrepancies between training and inference. items.title: Towards Value-Constrained Credit Assignment in Fully Delegated AI Cooperatives
items.summary: We propose a framework for reward allocation in fully delegated AI cooperatives where humans are represented by agents that contribute data and participate in model updates under heterogeneous value constraints. The key idea is to credit only those updates that remain admissible after screening them

items.title: HAT-4D: Lifting Monocular Video for 4D Multi-Object Interactions via Human-Agent Collaboration
items.summary: Extracting dynamic 4D object interactions from massive, in-the-wild monocular videos offers a highly efficient data collection pathway for scaling Embodied AI and training VLAs. items.title: The Remittance Blueprint: Data-driven Intelligence for Sri Lanka
items.summary: This study analyzes Sri Lankan migration and remittances over 32 years (1994-2025). Using a 384-month harmonized dataset, we apply exploratory data analysis, stationarity corrected time-series modeling (ADF, Johansen, VAR/VECM), and supervised learning. items.title: Cognitive Episodes in LLM Reasoning Traces Enable Interpretable Human Item Difficulty Prediction
items.summary: Predicting human item difficulty is central to educational assessment, where reliable estimates support fairness and effective test construction. items.title: CPAgents: Agentic Composite Phenotype Generation for Cardiac Disease Association

items.title: Tandem Reinforcement Learning with Verifiable Rewards

items.title: Robust Harmful Features Under Jailbreak Attacks: Mechanistic Evidence from Attention Head Specialization in Large Language Models
items.summary: Jailbreak attacks bypass LLM safety alignment, yet their mechanisms remain poorly understood. We provide evidence that attacks do not comprehensively eliminate safety features, but instead selectively suppress specific attention heads. items.title: Toward Robust In-Context Segmentation via Concept Guidance
items.summary: In-context segmentation (ICS) requires a model to segment target regions in a query image using only a few reference images and their corresponding masks, without updating any parameters. items.title: Beyond Sparse Supervision: Diffusion-Guided Learning for Few-Shot Graph Fraud Detection

items.title: Surprises in Proper Positive-Only Learning
items.summary: Binary classification from positive-only samples is a variant of PAC learning in which the learner receives i.i.d. samples from the positive region of an unknown target concept, but is evaluated under the original distribution (which places mass on both positive and negative regions). items.title: Second-Order KKT Guarantees for Bregman ADMM in Nonconvex and Non-Lipschitz Optimization
items.summary: We analyze Bregman ADMM for nonconvex linearly constrained problems under two-sided relative smoothness, a condition that replaces the standard Lipschitz gradient assumption with a Hessian comparison relative to a Bregman kernel. items.title: VGB for Masked Diffusion Model: Efficient Test-time Scaling for Reward Satisfaction and Sample Editing
items.summary: Inference-time scaling is a promising paradigm to improve generative models, especially when outputs must satisfy structural constraints or optimize downstream rewards. items.title: Democratic ICAI: Debating Our Way to Steering Principles from Preferences
items.summary: Preference-based alignment often struggles to capture the reasoning that underlies human judgments. items.title: Bridging Ab Initio Symmetries and Global Nuclear Masses with Interpretable Neural Networks
items.summary: Ab initio modeling has established Wigner's SU(4) and Elliott's SU(3) as dominant symmetries of the nuclear force in light and intermediate-mass nuclei. items.title: PAC-Bayesian Certificates for Quadratic Closed-Loop Control
